DESC: [IC-07] OPTIMIZED_PROMPT claims install_certificate verification is UNtestable on plain MQTT; it was exercised successfully on plain MQTT — The prompt states three times that 'install_certificate verification' (alongside MQTT_TLS / securityParams / VERIFY_PEER) 'remain UNtestable here / on this plain-1883 endpoint -> still schema/mock/blocker'. Live, install_certificate WAS successfully exercised over plain MQTT (no TLS) for BOTH certSource=HTTP and certSource=DIRECT, returning code 1 'Command payload is accepted' (apiVersion V1.21), and the install was independently verified via a get_installed_certificates read-back (code 0, ca_cert + client_cert enumerated). The prompt conflates the TLS transport/connection path (genuinely untestable on 1883) with the install_certificate command and its verificationType field, which are in fact testable and were verified on-device. This wrongly routes a verifiable command to the blocker/mock bucket.
EXPECTED: OPTIMIZED_PROMPT.md:13 (verbatim) — 'All MQTT_TLS / `securityParams` / verification paths (`VERIFY_PEER` etc., `install_certificate` verification) remain UNtestable here → still schema/mock/blocker.' Also :147 ('...`install_certificate` verification paths remain UNtestable on this endpoint → stay schema/mock/blocker') and :279 ('`install_certificate` verification remain UNtestable on this plain-1883 endpoint → stay schema/mock/blocker').
ACTUAL: install_certificate executed live on plain MQTT (port 1883, no TLS): SUCCESS/ACK {"response":{"code":1,"description":"Command payload is accepted"}} for both HTTP and DIRECT certSource across multiple requestIds; read-back get_installed_certificates returned code 0 'Success' and listed the installed ca_cert and client_cert. The command and its certificateBundle/certSource/verificationType handling are testable and were verified on plain MQTT.
REMEDIATION: Reword the three statements to scope 'UNtestable on plain MQTT' to the MQTT_TLS endpoint CONNECTION / securityParams transport path only. Acknowledge that the install_certificate COMMAND itself (HTTP and DIRECT certSource, certificateBundle, verificationType selection, and read-back via get_installed_certificates) IS exercisable and was [verified-on-device] over plain MQTT/1883. Move install_certificate out of the schema/mock/blocker-only bucket.
AUDIT: [WONT_FIX_OK] Dev STATUS='verified.' (a non-'Fixed' acknowledgement/disposition that the live install_certificate verification finding is real), NOT 'Open - not in v1' as the prior AUDIT wrongly stated. OPTIMIZED_PROMPT.md (last modified Jun 12, untouched by the 2026-06-30 resync) still lumps install_certificate verification into the 'UNtestable -> schema/mock/blocker' bucket alongside the TLS transport path at all three cited lines, verbatim: :13 'All MQTT_TLS / `securityParams` / verification paths (`VERIFY_PEER` etc., `install_certificate` verification) remain UNtestable here -> still schema/mock/blocker.'; :147 'All MQTT_TLS / `securityParams` ... / `verificationType` ... / `install_certificate` verification paths remain UNtestable on this endpoint -> stay schema/mock/blocker.'; :279 '`MQTT_TLS`/`securityParams`/`VERIFY_PEER`/`install_certificate` verification remain UNtestable on this plain-1883 endpoint -> stay schema/mock/blocker.' So the wording conflating the install_certificate command with the TLS transport path is unchanged on disk (divergence text still present), but dev has dispositioned the row 'verified.' (acknowledged, not a Fixed/Fixed2 fix-claim) -> WONT_FIX_OK, not STILL_PRESENT and not STATUS_MISMATCH. Doc-only file.
